tender.title: Trevone House - Semi Independent Accommodation for Young People 2020-2027
tender.description:
Trevone House in Gloucester will provide semi-independent accommodation, as well as other specialist services, that will help young people by giving them the support they need as they head into adulthood and find their independence.

We are looking for an exceptional provider to work with us to transform lives and help our young people move into independence. The provider will manage Trevone House, providing semi-independent accommodation for up to 19 young people aged 16 plus, the Staying Close service for those moving on and provide preventative Edge of Care wrap around services. The building also includes a designated space for day provision for the Council run Intensive Recovery Intervention Service and special Health and Wellbeing accommodation.

Please note The Services required under this procurement fall within Schedule 3 of the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 (SI 2015/102) and is therefore not bound by all of its requirements. Consequently, the procurement process adopted by the Commissioners has been developed in accordance with Regulation 76 of the regulations and in particular in accordance with the fundamental public procurement principles of transparency and equal treatment. The process used will be a restricted, modified competitive dialogue, information on this and the minimum requirements are detailed in the ITT documents. These are available by expressing an interest in this opportunity through www.supplyingthesouthwest.org.uk. In addition to the core contract with a estimated maximum value of £10.8M over the entire contract period (5 years +2 year optional extension), the OJEU estimated value takes into consideration potential additional support which may be required to meet the needs of residents over the contract lifetime.

Additional information: Please see updated closure date, description. Amended contract documents in ITT pack

tender.title: Supported Accommodation for Younger Adults in North Nottinghamshire
tender.description:
Nottinghamshire County Council is seeking tenders for the provision of a low level, short term, supported accommodation service in North Nottinghamshire (Mansfield, Ashfield, Newark & Sherwood and Bassetlaw areas). 
This contract replaces existing service contracts which are expiring.
The tender is for the provision of a support only contract, but that support will be delivered to people living in specific, designated units of accommodation. The Provider will support people aged 18 and over who have a Learning Disability, Mental Health condition, Autistic Spectrum Disorder (ASD) or a Physical Disability, in a minimum of 62 units of accommodation.  They will work with them to achieve a more stable lifestyle and develop the necessary independent living skills and confidence to sustain independent accommodation on a long term basis.  It is anticipated that people will remain in the Service for not more than 6 months.

tender.title: Northamptonshire Independent Supported Accommodation and Support Services for Young People Aged 16-18
tender.description: Northamptonshire County Council (Children First Northamptonshire) is setting up a Framework Agreement for the provision of supported accommodation services for looked after young people aged 16 - 18 years. The Framework Agreement is split into four (4) Lots: • Lot 1: Semi Supported Accommodation with 24 hour staffing (Accommodation Manager) and a minimum of 2 hours of 1:1 support per Young Person per week • Lot 2: Semi Independent Accommodation (solo or shared) with 2 hours of 1:1 Support per Young Person per week • Lot 3: Standard Additional Support Hours • Lot 4: Specialist Additional Support Hours. Potential Providers can tender for one, several or all Lots; however if they are tendering for Lot 1 or 2, they have to tender for Lot 3. Potential Providers can tender for Lot 3 and 4 only. The Framework Agreement is advertised under the Light Touch Regime (LTR) and will therefore be re-opened at regular intervals. The Council expects the Framework Agreement to re-open annually, but it may open at other intervals at its discretion.
